title: Operator Algebras Attack: Spectral Theory and Δd=2

spectral.note: log(A)(EML-2) = spectral measure(EML-0) integrated against log(EML-2): Δd=2

trace_class.note: Trace = EML-2: operator(EML-0) → Tr(A)(EML-2) = Δd=2 via counting measure

spectral_theorem.key_insight: Spectral theorem: matrix(EML-0) → log(A)(EML-2) = Δd=2; Trace = Δd=2 via counting measure

gelfand.measure_introduced: Haar measure on spectrum (maximal ideal space)
gelfand.conjecture_check: YES — Haar measure is introduced on the spectrum
gelfand.note: Gelfand: abstract C*-alg(EML-0) → C(Spec)(EML-2) = Δd=2; Haar measure

von_neumann.measure_introduced: KMS state (thermal measure at inverse temperature β)
von_neumann.note: Von Neumann: algebra(EML-0) → modular operator(EML-2) = Δd=2; KMS=thermal measure

gelfand_transform.key_insight: Gelfand: abstract(0)→C(Spec)(2)=Δd=2; modular theory: algebra(0)→modular op(2)=Δd=2

resolvent_formula.note: (A-λ)^{-1}: EML-1 (exp-decay in λ for dissipative A)

depth_table.pattern: All EML-2 operator objects involve trace/log: measure integration layer
depth_table.key_insight: Operator algebras: EML-0=abstract; EML-2=trace/log layer; EML-3=modular flow

eml_depth_summary.EML-2: log(A), Tr(A), von Neumann entropy, Gelfand transform, modular Δ, KMS
eml_depth_summary.EML-\u221e: Inductive limits of C*-algebras, non-commutative geometry

key_theorem: The EML Operator Algebra Theorem (S214): The spectral theorem provides the CANONICAL Δd=2 mechanism in operator theory: abstract operator A (EML-0) → log(A) = ∫log(λ)dE(λ) (EML-2): Δd=2. Trace Tr(A) = Σ⟨e_i,Ae_i⟩ = EML-2 (counting measure on ONB). Gelfand transform: C*-algebra (EML-0) → C(Spec)(EML-2): Δd=2. Modular theory: algebra (EML-0) → modular operator Δ (EML-2): Δd=2. Unified: Δd=2 in operator algebras = ALWAYS the 'trace/log' layer = integration against a spectral or counting measure.
rabbit_hole_log: Spectral theorem = canonical Δd=2: log(A) integrates EML-0 operator against spectral measure
Trace = EML-2: ONB counting measure makes Tr(A) an integral — Δd=2
Modular flow = EML-3: oscillatory in t makes σ_t the EML-3 layer above modular Δ
